creator: fuz@FreeBSD.org
text: These notes aside: this memory usage is far from the norm for compiling a C++ source file.  I believe there must be a bug in clang or llvm or some unfortunate design in the source file itself that causes this memory usage.  This is more than twice the highest memory usage I observed before (roughly 800 MB for one of the X86 instruction selection files in the LLVM source) and as opposed to that case, there is no apparent explanation for the memory usage.
